definition: Intel's rebranded server CPU line that splits into two families: P-core (performance) and E-core (efficiency/density), letting customers pick per workload while sharing a platform.

P-cores maximize per-core performance for latency-sensitive / licensed workloads; E-cores maximize core count and efficiency for scale-out and cloud-native density. Intel ships them as separate Xeon 6 lines.
Intel's 4th Gen Xeon Scalable — first with built-in accelerators (AMX for AI, DSA, QAT), DDR5, PCIe 5.0, and CXL 1.1 support. Also offered a rare HBM variant (Xeon Max).
Refinement of Sapphire Rapids: more cores, larger cache, higher efficiency on the same platform — an incremental performance-per-watt uplift.
Intel's high-performance Xeon 6 P-core CPU on Intel 3 process, with high core counts and support for faster memory including MRDIMM — aimed at demanding compute and AI head-node roles.
Intel's efficiency-core Xeon 6 with very high core counts (up to 288) for cloud-native, scale-out, high-density workloads where throughput-per-watt and rack consolidation matter.
Intel's next E-core Xeon, notable as an early high-volume server product on the Intel 18A process with backside power (PowerVia) and RibbonFET — a key manufacturing milestone.
Intel's next-generation P-core Xeon following Granite Rapids, expected to push higher core counts, new instructions (APX/AVX10), and a new platform.
Advanced Matrix Extensions — CPU instructions (tile-based matrix ops) that accelerate AI inference directly on Xeon, letting CPUs handle modest AI without a GPU.
Intel's dedicated AI training/inference accelerator (from Habana Labs), positioned as a lower-cost alternative to NVIDIA GPUs, using standard Ethernet for scale-out.
Intel's leading-edge (1.8nm-class) process node featuring RibbonFET (gate-all-around) transistors and PowerVia backside power delivery — central to Intel's foundry comeback.
Intel's 3D XPoint persistent memory that sat between DRAM and SSD in speed. Now discontinued — often cited as the market gap that CXL memory tiering aims to fill.
